evidence_quotes.quote: Digital Games as a service have EULAs that state that the game publisher can end the support for games and even go so far as to force the customer to destroy his already bought game, if the publisher decides to end his support.

Digital Games as a service have EULAs that state that the game publisher can end the support for games and even go so far as to force the customer to destroy his already bought game, if the publisher decides to end his support.

paragraphs.text: I would be very happy if the EU could add another topic to this initiative: Digital Games as a service have EULAs that state that the game publisher can end the support for games and even go so far as to force the customer to destroy his already bought game, if the publisher decides to end his support. I would like the EU to force publishers to have an end of life plan for games, so they dont have the right to destroy games that I have already bought and that should be my property.
